text.title: enum-constraints-closed-vocabularies Failure Modes
text.summary: Four failure modes with symptom, root cause, and repair.

section.title: Antipatterns

antipattern.description: Listing valid values inside the prompt or `description` while the field type stays `str`.
antipattern.reason: Soft constraint. The model mostly obeys but drifts under long context, low-temperature variation, or smaller models. A single wrong label silently breaks the downstream switch/case.

section.p: Symptom: 1-5% of production rows have labels not in the canonical list. Root cause: decoder is not masking. Fix: convert to `Literal[...]` / `enum`; enable strict mode.

antipattern.description: Trusting JSON-mode output to respect an enum without post-validation.
antipattern.reason: JSON mode shows the schema to the model but never enforces it via the decoder. Mini/nano models hallucinate enum values.

section.p: Symptom: validator-retry rate is high on the JSON-mode path. Root cause: no enforcement. Fix: upgrade to strict mode on this provider, or add retry-with-feedback / map-to-nearest in the orchestrator.

antipattern.description: Modelling semi-open sets as `Literal["a","b","c"] | str` directly.
antipattern.reason: The union collapses to `str` in strict mode; the enum constraint is silently lost.

section.p: Symptom: every value reaches downstream as plain `str`. Root cause: wrong union shape. Fix: use a discriminated union with `kind: Literal["known"|"novel"]`.

antipattern.description: Enum values that are case-sensitive in the schema but where the prompt examples use mixed case ("Billing" vs "billing").
antipattern.reason: Strict mode still accepts only exact-match tokens; mixed-case examples confuse the model and lead to validator failures.

section.p: Symptom: validator rejects ~5% of outputs with case mismatches. Root cause: inconsistent casing across schema + prompt. Fix: pick lowercase snake_case across schema, prompts, and DB; document the convention; add a normaliser as a safety net.
